summary_text: **Findings** - `role_weights` is defined in config but not used by runtime voting today. [lane-guard.json](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/docs/lane-guard.json#L68) - `lane-guard.js` has a working accessor (`getRoleWeight`) but that module is effectively unused by consensus paths. [lane-guard.js](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/lane-guard.js#L93) - Consensus has weighting, but from a different source (`quality-gates.kernel.weights`) and only a coarse split (`security` vs `factual`), not agent-role weights. [consensus-vote.js](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/consensus-vote.js#L327) [consensus-vote.js](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/consensus-vote.js#L340) - Default voting path is unweighted majority; weighted logic is judge-mode only. [consensus-vote.js](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/consensus-vote.js#L388) - Test coverage does not verify role-weight behavior at all (only voter invocation wiring). [test-phase1-direct-callers.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-phase1-direct-callers.mjs#L94) **Status** - Overall: **partially wired** - Specifically for `lane-guard.json.role_weights`: **dead config** in current vote runtime. **Smallest coherent implementation target** 1. Wire `informedJudge` to use `getRoleWeight(agent)` from `lane-guard.js` for per-voter weight (fallback to existing kernel weights if missing). 2. Export `informedJudge` for direct unit testing. 3. Add one focused test validating that changing `role_weights` changes `weightSummary` and verdict behavior in judge mode. This is the minimum change that makes `role_weights` live without redesigning the whole consensus system.
